The pot on the fire represented Jerusalem besieged by the Chaldeans: all ranks were within the walls, prepared as a prey for the enemy. They should have put away their transgressions, just as scum that rises with the heat is taken from the top of the pot. But they grew worse, and their miseries increased. Jerusalem was to be leveled with the ground. The time appointed for the punishment of wicked people may seem to come slowly, but it will surely come. It is sad to think how many there are on whom ordinances and providences are all lost.

Though mourning for the dead is a duty, it must be controlled by religion and right reason: we must not sorrow as those who have no hope. Believers must not copy the language and expressions of those who do not know God. The people asked about the meaning of the sign. God takes from them all that was dearest to them. And just as Ezekiel did not weep for his affliction, neither should they weep for theirs. Blessed be God, we need not pine away under our afflictions; for even if all comforts fail and all sorrows are united, yet the broken heart and the mourner’s prayer are always acceptable before God.
